meaning: adj.不适于食用的,不能吃的
examples: Detainees complained of being given inedible food.
They don't waste their time on some inedible thing from their point of view.
That would allow a restaurant critic, for example, to describe food as revolting and inedible, without having to prove it.
Using inedible inputs avoids fights about diverting food crops for fuel, and frees the industry from reliance on a single commodity.
Pyle says that even biofuels made of inedible crops are using up valuable resources which would be better spent producing food.
